text: Add editor support for creating and editing tickets
Editor resolution order: $VISUAL, then $EDITOR, then fallback to 'vim'. The only correct answer is vim, but we should allow others too.

On validation-failure the user should have the option to abort editing and revert, in case he can't figure out the problem or wants to drop his changes. Abort condition could e.g. be to write an empty file.

spec is solid (two notes). Key implementation notes: use tempfile + /vim/vim; validate all fields before commit; on validation failure offer re-edit not discard; empty file = abort. Non-trivial but self-contained. Human-facing so p2 is right.

Worth reusing any validation/error machinery from T-82 and T-77 if those land first. The editor flow is where low-level validation failures become very human-facing, so shared validators plus friendly error formatting would keep behavior consistent.
honestly I think this whole feature is overkill. everything can easily be done from the commandline. no need to add extra paths and requirements for syntax testing.
